name: OGL (Minimal WebGL)
description: Minimal zero-dependency WebGL library — Three.js-like API at 15kb. Raw shader access for noise displacement, fluid simulations, custom vertex shaders, post-processing. ES module, no build step.
body:
<script type="module">
import { Renderer, Camera, Transform, Geometry, Program, Mesh } from 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/ogl/src/index.mjs';

(function() {
  var container = document.querySelector('.ogl-canvas-container');
  if (!container) return;

  var renderer = new Renderer({ alpha: true, antialias: true });
  var gl = renderer.gl;
  container.appendChild(gl.canvas);
  gl.canvas.style.cssText = 'position:absolute;top:0;left:0;width:100%;height:100%;pointer-events:none;';

  var camera = new Camera(gl, { fov: 35 });
  camera.position.set(0, 0, 5);

  function resize() { renderer.setSize(container.offsetWidth, container.offsetHeight); camera.perspective({ aspect: gl.canvas.width / gl.canvas.height }); }
  window.addEventListener('resize', resize);
  resize();

  var scene = new Transform();
  // Add your custom geometry/shaders here
  // See https://oframe.github.io/ogl/examples for full examples

  requestAnimationFrame(function update(t) {
    requestAnimationFrame(update);
    renderer.render({ scene: scene, camera: camera });
  });
})();
</script>
